codeReview.summary: Actions and sync routes are mostly implemented and mounted correctly, but malformed pagination parameters are not validated and can bypass 400 handling, violating the feature's invalid-params behavior requirement.

issues.description: GET /api/actions parses limit/offset with parseInt but never validates NaN/negative values. Invalid inputs (e.g., limit=abc, offset=-1) are forwarded to storage.getActions and can surface as 500/database errors instead of 400, which violates expected behavior that invalid params return 400.

PATCH /api/actions/:id converts snoozedUntil via new Date(...) without validating parseability, and does not type-check priority/reason. Malformed bodies can fall through to storage and fail as 500s instead of clean 400 validation errors.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill requires RED->GREEN (tests before implementation) and manual API verification, but this run implemented routes before creating tests and reported no manual endpoint verification while still marking followedProcedure=true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl line 32 shows Create server/routes/actions.ts before Create server/__tests__/phase2-actions-api.test.ts; handoff.verification only lists npm run check and vitest commands; backend-worker/SKILL.md lines 33-41 and 53-60 require tests-first and manual API checks.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission AGENTS mandates Zod validation for API routes, but the actions router uses ad-hoc checks and typed casts instead of schema validation; this likely contributed to missed invalid-input handling.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: mission AGENTS.md line 66 says 'Use Zod for validation'; server/routes/actions.ts lines 44-60 and 128-155 implement manual validation/casting without Zod schemas.

summary: Review failed due to a blocking input-validation gap: invalid pagination params are not rejected with 400. Non-blocking gap: PATCH body lacks robust validation for malformed values. Shared-state observations noted around skill-procedure compliance and validation-convention enforcement.
